description:
Goal
Route all compiler2 native list construction through the generalized reusable-cons capability and delete the current tail-only codegen path.

Why
`src/compiler2/native_codegen/prim.rs` currently tries `emit_owned_cons_reuse_or_alloc` for one-element `MakeList` and `DestListCons`, but that helper only calls the tail-relink primitive. After the IR/runtime model is generalized, codegen should have exactly one reuse consumption path: if the head being consed has a reusable source, call the generalized runtime primitive with source cons, new head, head kind, and new tail; otherwise allocate normally.

Algorithm
- In codegen cache construction (`src/compiler2/native_codegen/function.rs` / `env.rs`), index reusable-cons facts by rebuilt head var using the new IR capability names.
- Replace `emit_owned_cons_reuse_or_alloc` with a generalized helper such as `emit_reusable_cons_or_alloc`.
- The helper must:
  - find the physical source cons for the consed head var;
  - compute the runtime representation of the new head exactly as ordinary `emit_list_cons_bif` would;
  - compute/pass the head kind expected by runtime;
  - compute list tail bits exactly as ordinary allocation does;
  - call the generalized runtime primitive;
  - return `None` when no reusable source exists so ordinary allocation remains the only fallback.
- Apply this helper to both:
  - `Prim::MakeList(elems, Some(tail))` when `elems.len() == 1`;
  - `Prim::DestListCons { head, tail, .. }`.
- Multi-element literals should continue to build from the tail backward; only the individual cons operation with a reusable head/source uses the primitive.

Deletion target
- Delete `emit_owned_cons_reuse_or_alloc`.
- Delete `CodegenFn::list_reuse_or_cons_tail_ref` and runtime binding ids once no caller remains.
- Delete `fz_list_reuse_or_cons_tail_ref` and heap tail-only helpers if no non-duplicative runtime test/user remains.
- Remove old `owned_cons_reuse_sources` naming from codegen caches.

Tests
- Add/update native codegen tests for same-head rebuild and changed-head rebuild. The changed-head case is essential; otherwise the implementation may still be tail-only in disguise.
- Use `--log-telemetry` while debugging `fixtures2/behavior/enum_sort.fz` and `enum_map_family.fz` to ensure reuse consumption events appear.
- Run focused fixture tests for `enum_sort`, `enum_list_allocations`, `enum_map_family`, and any existing quicksort owned-cons reuse test.

Acceptance
Compiler2 native codegen has one generalized list-cons reuse consumption path. The tail-only primitive and old helper names are gone from production code.
title: Native codegen: consume reusable-cons facts for MakeList and DestListCons
